title_original: China likely to have standards for post-quantum cryptography in 3 years, expert says
summary_original: Wang Xiaoyun, a professor at Tsinghua University and delegate to China’s National People’s Congress, stated that China will likely establish national standards for post-quantum cryptography within three years, prioritizing the finance and energy sectors. This comes as China’s new five-year plan designates quantum technology as a strategic industry and seeks to develop a scalable quantum computer. Unlike United States and international researchers, Chinese efforts focus on “structureless lattice” algorithms like S-Cloud+, which Wang claims avoid “security degradation” present in some international standards. Last month, China released an open-source operating system for quantum computers. The United States finalized its first post-quantum cryptography standards in 2024 and targets full industry migration by 2035, with similar timelines in South Korea.
